ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Real Estate and Property Management
ProfessionalRecognition:
The programme is recognized by the following organizations:

Property Management Services Authority - Specified Qualifications for the Property Management Practitioner (Level 2) Licence: Graduates are eligible to apply for the Property Management Practitioner (Level 2) Licence with two years of accumulated property management experience or one year of such experience with the supervision of Property Management Practitioner (Level 1) Licence holder.
Chartered Institute of Housing Asian Pacific Branch — Recognized Course for the Membership: Graduates are eligible to apply for being their Member with the completion of Practical Experience Requirements (PER).
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BSc (Hons) in Surveying (Year 3 Entry)^ Graduates may pursue relevant Degrees locally or overseas. They may also apply for admission to the top-up Degree programmes in Hong Kong offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): Northumbria University, UK: BSc (Hons) in Global Business Management # [Not applicable to Mainland students] ^ Students may be required to take bridging modules and provide English proficiency proof (Admission requirements are subject to change; Kindly contact THEi for details) # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed to take up the roles on the supervisory level, ranging from Assistant Property Officer, Officer Trainee, Facilities Management Officer, Property Officer, Building Maintenance Officer to Property / Portfolio in-charge. For those with experience or other qualifications, they may take up higher positions.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Economics , Fundamentals of Management , Quantitative Analysis for Business , Landscape Planning and Building Materials , Contemporary Issues for Housing Management , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversion and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , IT Essentials - Business , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness
Curriculum.Semester 2: Accounting , Marketing Essentials , ESG and PropTech in Action , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , AI and IoT for Property Management Services , Professional Ethics and Supervisory Management , Work-integrated Learning * , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Building Services , Agency and Conveyancing , Customer Experience Enhancement , Work-integrated Learning * , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching & Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 4: Business and Property Law , Real Estate Development & Valuation , Building Maintenance & Enhancement in Practice , Facilities Management & Environmental Protection in Practice , Work-integrated Learning * , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
Curriculum.Semester 5: Property Management & Risk Assessment in Practice , Intelligent Building Systems & Building Information Modelling , Final Year Project , Corporate Placement , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling & Job Search , Enrichment Module B

StudentSharing: The programme provides practical knowledge and skills training, covering various areas of property management to establish a strong academic foundation. The internship provided me with valuable real-world insights into property management, enabling me to navigate diverse challenges and explore potential career paths within the industry. This experience has boosted my confidence as I prepare for my future career.

StudentSharing.Wong Wing Yan: Manager (Facilities Management / Leasing), New World Facilities Management Company Limited

StudentSharing: I am so grateful for the all-round training in my 2-year study in IVE. It offered me the solid knowledge that is crucial to a professional in the real estate industry.

StudentSharing.Lam Tsz King: Leasing Officer, Central Plaza Management Co. Ltd.

They may also apply for admission to the top-up Degree programmes in Hong Kong offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): Northumbria University, UK: BSc (Hons) in Global Business Management # [Not applicable to Mainland students] # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed in the fields of advertising / PR, marketing, and media. In advertising, media and PR firms, they may be employed as Advertising Officers, Account Executives, Designers, PR Officers, Social Media Content Developers, Event Planners, Youtubers, etc. In the marketing field, graduates may be employed as Marketing Officers or Brand Executives. The career nature is challenging and highly diversified.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Advertising Studio 1: Digital Content Creation, Graphic Design and Photography , Creative Cultures in Advertising Marketing Communications , Marketing Essentials , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Business
Curriculum.Semester 2: Advertising Studio 2: Design and Development of Interactive Media , Quantitative Analysis for Business Digital Marketing Basics , Fundamentals of Management , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Enrichment Module A , Character Design with Intellectual Property Management , Legal Issues in Marketing and Advertising , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
Curriculum.Semester 4: Media Planning and Digital Advertising Strategy , Public Relations and Public Speaking , Consumer Behaviour , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Final Year Project , Work-integrated Learning*
Curriculum.Semester 5: Advertising Media Production , Brand Storytelling and Media Writing , Experiential Marketing and Event Management Marketing Strategy , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module B , Work-integrated Learning*

StudentSharing: The course content is in line with the environment of the advertising industry and keeps pace with the times; The theory and skills can be applied in the real world. My knowledge of advertising and marketing has helped me to be proficient in media planning.

Looking back in the past few years, from the Foundation Diploma to the Higher Diploma in Advertising and Media Communications, I have not only found what I want and expect, but also made a group of like-minded friends while accumulating knowledge. From the photography skills, color matching and multimedia operations I learned in class, to the application of these skills in my internship at Ocean Park, it helped me to become proficient in these knowledge and skills. I am also glad that I have met many photographer friends and have even been invited to participate in their personal shooting. These different experiences have enriched my resume and paved the way for further studies and career in the future.

StudentSharing.Lo Man Yee: Student, Higher Diploma in Advertising and Media Communications, IVE

StudentSharing: During my studies, I was assigned an internship with a fashion brand and participated in the advertising production process. This experience deepened my understanding of the advertising industry and greatly supported my transition into the emerging digital marketing field.

StudentSharing: The HRM programme has not only enabled me to learn hands-on HR techniques but also to build a solid foundation to face the business setting after graduation.The well-structured programme and professional teaching crew have inspired and broadened my interest in the HRM field. Till now, the knowledge which I equipped still profoundly imprinted in my daily work as a learning specialist.During my studies, I have had the opportunity to participate in the pilot internship programme, which further enriches my understanding of how to be and what should be a competent HR practitioner.Sincerely gratitude to the program and teachers of HRM, and I deeply believe you won’t regret on choosing IVE HRM program.

This programme has equipped me with necessary expertise to work as an HR professional. The curriculum covers areas such as talent and compensation management, training, talent analytics, and relevant laws.  Additionally, the course explores the practical applications of cutting-edge technologies like artificial intelligence and virtual reality in the field of human resource management, keeping up with the latest technological developments.  Throughout the course, I completed various assignments aimed at simulating actual human resource management work in a company.  These assignments involved tasks such as developing employee training programs, fostering employee relations, and recruiting talent. Through these assignments, I gained a deeper understanding of the responsibilities of HR managers. This experience made me realize my genuine interest in these tasks and further inspired my desire to pursue relevant courses in university and embark on a career in human resource management.

StudentSharing.Chan Yee Ting: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Human Resources & Talent Analytics, IVE

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Marketing and Customer Analytics
OfferingCampusOrVenue: IVE (Kwun Tong / Sha Tin / Tuen Mun)
StudyPlaces: IVE (Kwun Tong) - 25 , IVE (Sha Tin) - 30 , IVE (Tuen Mun) - 25
ProfessionalRecognition: Students may join the Hong Kong Institute of Marketing as Student Member.
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BA (Hons) in Digital Advertising BA (Hons) in Public Relations and International Events Management Graduates may pursue relevant Degrees locally or overseas. They may also apply for admission to the top-up Degree programmes in Hong Kong offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): Northumbria University, UK: BSc (Hons) in Global Business Management # [Not applicable to Mainland students] # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates are competent to take up positions in a wide range of marketing fields in public and private sectors. These include Marketing Executive, Marketing Planning Coordinator, Digital Marketing Executive, Data Analyst, Marketing Communications Officer, Advertising Account Executive, Business Development Executive, Sales Executive, Sales and Marketing Coordinator, Brand Management Executive, Marketing Research Officer, Event Planning Executive, Public Relations Executive and Customer Services Officer.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Marketing Essentials , Mobile and Social Media Marketing , Economics , Accounting , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Business
Curriculum.Semester 2: Buyer Behaviour , Quantitative Analysis for Business , Fundamentals of Management , Digital Marketing Skills and Strategy , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Marketing Research , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 4: Legal Aspects for Marketers , Marketing Management , Customer Data Analytics , Public Relations and Event Planning , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Final Year Project , Work-integrated Learning*
Curriculum.Semester 5: Marketing Communications and Customer Relationship Management , Marketing Innovation and Technology , Brands and Green Business Practices , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Final Year Project , Enrichment Module B , Work-integrated Learning*

StudentSharing: The programme strengthens my foundational knowledge, enhances my understanding and application of industry concepts. This better prepares me for university, and ultimately equips me for future employment.

StudentSharing.Leung Chun Yeung: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Marketing and Customer Analytics, IVE

StudentSharing: The programme not only provided me the understanding of the marketing theories, but also trained me to apply the knowledge practically through projects and internship.  IVE equipped me to be well-prepared for my development in the marketing profession.

StudentSharing.CHEN Qilina: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Marketing and Customer Analytics, IVE

Curriculum.Semester 1: Economics , Marketing Essentials , Principles and Practices of Financial Planning , Principles of Insurance , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Business , Work-integrated Learning*
Curriculum.Semester 2: Accounting , Quantitative Analysis for Business , Banking , Financial Markets , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement , Work-integrated Learning*

Curriculum.Semester 4: Fundamentals of Management , Insurance Products and Practices , Derivatives and Fund Management , Securities and Futures Regulation , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Final Year Project , Enrichment Module B , Work-integrated Learning*
Curriculum.Semester 5: Accounting in Banking and Finance , Risk Management , Retirement and Estate Planning , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Final Year Project , Work-integrated Learning*

StudentSharing: I found the Higher Diploma program of IVE very useful for developing and strengthening my professional knowledge in banking and wealth management. Through the work-integrated learning program, I had an opportunity to participate in the internship programme of a financial institution. I learned practical knowledge about financial planning and communication skills which would, in turn, raise my learning effectiveness, skills in managing changes, and confidence. This was a valuable experience that could not be learnt from books.

StudentSharing.Kong Kin Ming: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Banking and Financial Analysis, IVE

ProfessionalRecognition: The programme has established closed relationship with business enterprises and professional bodies. Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates are equipped to be employed in logistics and aviation management under different types of organisations including logistics companies, international express courier companies and airlines. Graduates may be employed as logistics officers, multinational courier company operations officers, warehouse operations officers, transportation operations officers, aviation operations assistants, etc.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Marketing Essentials , Introduction to Aviation Industry , Service Management , Business Environment for Transport and Logistics , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth and Effectiveness , Information Technology Essentials – Business
Curriculum.Semester 2: Quantitative Analysis for Business , Fundamentals of Management , Logistics Management for e-Commerce Operations , Introduction to Air Cargo Operations , Human Resource Management in Aviation , Live Commerce with Design Thinking , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence
Curriculum.Semester 3: Special Cargo Handling , SMART Airport Operations , Global Trade Practices
Curriculum.Semester 4: Contemporary Issues on Aviation and Logistics , Law of Business and Carriage , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 5: Accounting , Shipping Practices , Safety and Security in Aviation , SMART Warehousing , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement , Enrichment Module A

StudentSharing: During my two-year study at IVE, I gained valuable experience in aviation management, logistics, and business law. My participation in the Singapore Study Tour, along with internships at Asia Airfreight Terminal in Hong Kong, significantly enriched my industry knowledge. Additionally, I attended numerous site and company visits, which deepened my understanding of industry operations. I am grateful to IVE for equipping me with both the theoretical knowledge and practical skills essential for success in the aviation and logistics industry.

StudentSharing.Wong Yi Wai: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Aviation and Logistics, IVE

StudentSharing: The learning model of this course effectively combines theory and practice. During my studies, I participated in Guangxi exchange tours and interned at an airfreight terminal. This experience provided me with valuable insights into current industry operations. By synthesizing my practical experience with the theoretical knowledge acquired in the course, I was able to excel in the workplace and received multiple job offers. I highly recommend this course to students interested in pursuing a career in the aviation and logistics industry.

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.

StudentSharing: Sham Tak Yan Carol, currently a Year 3 Marketing student at PolyU, has demonstrated exceptional potential in the business field. These experiences have thoroughly prepared her for a future in Business Management, showcasing the immense value of practical learning and international exposure provided by the Higher Diploma in Business Administration and Management program.

StudentSharing: The programmes of IVE are very useful to my career development. Foundation Diploma programmes helped me strengthen the language proficiency and arouse my interest in business studies. It also inspires me to choose accountancy as my lifelong profession. I was then articulated to Diploma/ Higher Diploma in Accountancy and Information Systems and was taught by wonderful lecturers. I am now a professional accountant and am greatly indebted to them for their teaching.

StudentSharing.Lau Man Kei: Director, Consultation and Solution Hatcher Group Limited (a company listed on the Hong Kong Exchange)

StudentSharing: The lecturers in IVE are professional and caring.  They offered me a wide variety of activities which enriched my learning journey.  Such learning activities made me experience how technology and innovation helped the accountancy profession.  IVE also provided me with a solid foundation of accounting knowledge which are useful in my career.    After graduated from IVE, I continued my study in Bachelor of Business Administration (Hons) in Accountancy at the Hong Kong Polytechnic University and it is my honour to work in a sizable conglomerate now.

StudentSharing.Shao Zipeng: Senior Officer (Internal Audit), CITIC Pacific Limited

StudentSharing: During my time at IVE, I think my most valuable experience was the opportunity to work as a project coordinator for an international conference, as well as a PR and event planner for a charity event that spread the message of social inclusion. These invaluable experiences confirmed my decision to pursue a degree in Communications at Hong Kong Baptist University and my intention to pursue a career in PR and media.

StudentSharing: IVE emphasises  both theory and practice, it provides a clear study and career articulation path for students to achieve their dream. Based on students interest and ability, they can select their own study mode and learning content.

The Higher Diploma in Child Care and Education at IVE covers multiple professional areas. During my studies, I was assigned an internship at a kindergarten, where I applied various pedagogies into practice, helping me become a professional early childhood educator.

While studying at IVE, I was assigned internships at various types of organisations, where I gained exposure to early childhood education. After graduating and becoming a teacher, I frequently apply the knowledge and skills I acquired during my internships.

The Higher Diploma in Child Care and Education at IVE is recognised by the Education Bureau, enabling graduates to obtain registration qualifications as kindergarten teachers and special childcare workers. The comprehensive curriculum includes practical, workplace-relevant pedagogies such as play-based learning, the concepts and applications of which have been invaluable throughout my career.

StudentSharing.Shandy Cheung: Principal, Yuen Long Lutheran Life Kindergarten

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma in Child Care and Education at IVE covers multiple professional areas. During my studies, I was assigned an internship at a kindergarten, where I applied various pedagogies into practice, helping me become a professional early childhood educator.

While studying at IVE, I was assigned internships at various types of organisations, where I gained exposure to early childhood education. After graduating and becoming a teacher, I frequently apply the knowledge and skills I acquired during my internships.

The Higher Diploma in Child Care and Education at IVE is recognised by the Education Bureau, enabling graduates to obtain registration qualifications as kindergarten teachers and special childcare workers. The comprehensive curriculum includes practical, workplace-relevant pedagogies such as play-based learning, the concepts and applications of which have been invaluable throughout my career.

StudentSharing.Shandy Cheung: Principal, Yuen Long Lutheran Life Kindergarten

ProfessionalRecognition: Graduates are eligible for registration as health workers with the Social Welfare Department2.

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

This programme is only applicable to VTC Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education graduates who have completed Secondary 6 under New Senior Secondary Academic Structure (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
This programme is NOT applicable to VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate graduates.
Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to full-time / part-time Bachelor Degree programmes at various local and overseas universities, e.g.: BSc (Hons) in Health and Social Care, the University of the West of England, Bristol, UK (Registration No.: 253136) # BSc in Gerontology, the Chinese University of Hong Kong BA (Hons) in Applied Ageing Studies and Service Management, the Hong Kong Polytechnic University BA (Hons) in Social Policy and Social Entrepreneurship, the Hong Kong Polytechnic University BA (Hons) in Social Design, the Hong Kong Polytechnic University BSSc (Hons) in Social Entrepreneurship and Development Studies, the Education University of Hong Kong BSSc (Hons) in Innovation in Health and Social Well-Being, Hong Kong Baptist University # This 1-year full-time / 1.5-year part-time programme is offered in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE) of VTC. It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE .
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as health care officers, programme workers, rehabilitation assistants and administrative executives in the social welfare and elderly care services industries.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Introduction to Psychology , Sociology of Aging , Service Learning in Elderly Services , Smart Living and Techno-aging , Health and Wellbeing , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness
Curriculum.Semester 2: Health and Nursing Care I , Rehabilitative Care and Exercise for the Elderly , Group Work Practice for the Elderly , Social and Health Policy for Aging Society , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Programme Planning in Community Health , Health and Nursing Care II , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , IT Essentials - Services , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Research Studies in Elderly Services , Health and Nursing Care III , Working with Persons with Dementia , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module A , Elective (choose one of the following): - Social Enterprise and Business Management- Horticultural Practice for the Elderly- Elderly Fitness- Pre-retirement Planning- Non-Pharmacological Pain Management Therapies for Older Adults- Practice of Expressive Art for the Elderly
Curriculum.Semester 5: Fieldwork Placement (Residential Care Services for Older Adults) , Professional Ethics in Elderly Services , Hospice and Palliative Care , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing

StudentSharing: The programme helped me to gain a deeper understanding of the characteristics and needs of the elderly, and also equipped me with the qualifications of a registered health worker and the skills to lead the group activities for the elderly. I have the ability to progress to relevant courses and become a professional registered social worker.

StudentSharing.Tam Ka Man: Project Manger, Yang Memorial Methodist Social Service

StudentSharing: I chose VTC as it is a great place for my study and career. During the three years of study, I found my target and interest in elderly care. VTC provided many field experience and practicum training to me, and brought me to a deeper understanding of elderly care.

StudentSharing.Siu Wing Yi: Project Director, Caritas Rehabilitation Service Cartias Chan Chun Ma Day

They are eligible to apply for admission to the top-up Degree programme offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): University of the West of England, Bristol, UK: BA (Hons) in Graphic Design # (Registration Number of Non-local Higher and Professional Education: 253266) # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. StudentSharing: As a child, I already enjoyed sewing clothes. I turned my interest into a profession by enrolling at HKDI after my secondary education. This pathway led me to PolyU and aided me in progressively developing my fashion brand and taking it to the global stage!

StudentSharing.Toki Wong: Founder of Kowloon City Boy, a fashion brand

StudentSharing: Since I was young, I have enjoyed telling stories through images. Therefore, after graduating from secondary school, I enrolled in the HKDI's Higher Diploma in Film, Television and Photography.  After graduation, I had the opportunity to participate in the post-production of several local films, including Shock Wave 2 and Tyson Yoshi's That Guy music video. I was also involved in producing visual effects for the recently released film Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom.

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Visual Arts and Culture
ProfessionalRecognition: Students are eligible to apply for student membership of the Graphic Arts Association of Hong Kong (GAAHK) and the Hong Kong Designers Association (HKDA).
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may pursue relevant Degrees locally or overseas. They are eligible to apply for admission to the top-up Degree programme offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): University of the West of England, Bristol, UK: BA (Hons) in Fine Art # (Registration Number of Non-local Higher and Professional Education: 253254) # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE . Graduates may also apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes of local universities: Hong Kong Baptist University: BA (Hons) in Visual Arts Lingnan University: BA (Hons) in Visual Studies The Chinese University of Hong Kong: BA in Fine Arts The Education University of Hong Kong: BA (Hons) in Creative Arts and Culture (Visual Arts) The Hong Kong Polytechnic University: BA (Hons) in Design (Product Design) Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on an individual basis.
CareerProspects: Apart from professional artists, graduates will have full potential to take up other career opportunities. These include graphic designers, illustrators, photographers, exhibition designers, visual merchandisers, etc. They may also be art administrators at the assistant level in art centres, gallery, cultural and educational organisation to run cultural programmes or art exhibitions. The graduates are capable to be future entrepreneurs who can turn concepts into art entrepreneurial ideas or they can be curators, critics, educators, cultural programme administrators in the future.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Creative and Design Thinking , Visual Culture , Drawing , Photography and Moving Image , Three-dimensional Form and Materials , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Cultural Studies , Image Making , Video and Sound , Objects and Space , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part 1)
Curriculum.Semester 3: Aesthetics and Semiotics , Object Making , Mixed Media , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part 2)
Curriculum.Semester 4: Exhibitions and Curatorial Practices , Asian Art , Graduation Project Development , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 5: Cultural and Creative Design: Visual Arts and Culture , Graduation Project: Visual Arts and Culture , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module A

StudentSharing: Karma is the first professional and large-scale production that I participated in as a director. First of all, I would like to thank HKDI for providing me with such a special opportunity to be involved in such a large-scale film production. From writing the script from scratch, designing characters, scenes, props, and finally completing the shooting, the entire opportunity is absolutely priceless to me. The knowledge gained through hands-on practice during this process is invaluable.

StudentSharing.Tony Wong: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Animation and Visual Effects, HKDI

StudentSharing: As a child, I already enjoyed sewing clothes. I turned my interest into a profession by enrolling at HKDI after my secondary education. This pathway led me to PolyU and aided me in progressively developing my fashion brand and taking it to the global stage!

StudentSharing.Toki Wong: Founder of Kowloon City Boy, a fashion brand

StudentSharing: Since I was young, I have enjoyed telling stories through images. Therefore, after graduating from secondary school, I enrolled in the HKDI's Higher Diploma in Film, Television and Photography.  After graduation, I had the opportunity to participate in the post-production of several local films, including Shock Wave 2 and Tyson Yoshi's That Guy music video. I was also involved in producing visual effects for the recently released film Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom.

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in User Experience Design
ProfessionalRecognition:
Students are eligible to apply for student membership of the Hong Kong Designers Association (HKDA).


Students are eligible to apply for student membership of the Interaction Designers Association Hong Kong (IxDA HK).
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes of local universities: The Hong Kong Polytechnic University: BA (Hons) in Design (Information Design) The Hong Kong Polytechnic University: BA (Hons) in Design (Interaction Design) The Hong Kong Polytechnic University - SPEED: BA(Hons) in Media and Communication Design - Interaction Design Stream The Hong Kong Metropolitan University: BFA (Hons) in Computing and Interactive Entertainment Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on individual basis.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as user experience manager, digital product designer, frontend app and web designer, digital and entertainment designer, information and communication designer, user experience designer and user interface designer, in areas as diverse as graphic house, design consultant, branding, digital advertising, digital marketing solution, event and exhibitions, museum display and art / event promotion. Graduates usually work as individual design practitioners and design entrepreneurs setting up their own studios.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , Creative and Design Thinking , IT Essentials - Smart Living , Iconography Design , Information Graphic , UI Fundamental
Curriculum.Semester 2: Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part 1) , Cultural Studies , Web Application Interface Design , UI Motion Design
Curriculum.Semester 3: Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part 2) , Aesthetics and Sustainability , Tablet UI Design , Usability Testing and Analysis , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , UX Design for Museum and Exhibition , Immersive Experience Design , Graduation Project 1: User Experience Design , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 5: Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Prototyping , Graduation Project 2: User Experience Design

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Visual Communication Design
ProfessionalRecognition:
Students may choose one of the following streams so as to enhance professional knowledge and skills in specific areas:

Higher Diploma in Visual Communication Design
Higher Diploma in Visual Communication Design (Publishing Design and Print Media)

The Higher Diploma in Visual Communication Design also offers an Earn-and-Learn alternation scheme, enabling students to gain practical experience while studying:

Higher Diploma in Visual Communication Design (Earn and Learn scheme)

The first three semesters consist of a common curriculum, and students can decide to enter the Earn-and-Learn scheme in Semester 3. From Semesters 4 to 7, students will engage in part-time study within the Earn-and-Learn scheme (DE314116). Higher Diploma in Visual Communication Design (Earn and Learn scheme) from Semester 1 to Semester 7 can typically be completed in 3 years.

Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes offered by local or overseas universities. They are eligible to apply for admission to the top-up Degree programmes offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): University of the West of England, Bristol, UK: BA (Hons) Graphic Design # (Registration Number of Non-local Higher and Professional Education: 253266) University of the West of England, Bristol, UK: BA (Hons) in Illustration # (Registration Number of Non-local Higher and Professional Education: 253265) # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE . Graduates may apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes of local universities: City University of Hong Kong: BA in Creative Media Hong Kong Baptist University: BA (Hons) in Visual Arts The Hong Kong Polytechnic University: BA (Hons) in Design (Advertising Design) / BA (Hons) in Design (Information Design) / BA (Hons) in Design (Media Design) / BA (Hons) in Design (Social Design) Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on an individual basis.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as graphic designer, information designer, brand consultant, event / promotion art director, packaging designer, book designer, illustrator, storyboard artist and character designer, in areas as diverse as graphic house, design consultant, branding, advertising, magazine and publication media, book design, printing, packaging, digital marketing, event and exhibitions, illustration, environmental and spatial graphic, museum display, art / event promotion and image direction. Graduates frequently work as individual design practitioners and design entrepreneurs setting up their own studios.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Creative and Design Thinking , Digital Imaging and Motion , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Graphic Language , Typography: Expression and Information , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials – Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Cultural Studies , Studio: Design to Read , Photography for Visual Communication Design , Graphic Arts , Infographics: Diagrams and Data Graphics , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part 1) , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 3: Aesthetics and Sustainability , Infographics: Maps and Instructional Illustrations , Packaging Design , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part 2) , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Graduation Project Development for Visual Communication Design , Studio: Design to Promote , Visual Identity System , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing
Curriculum.Semester 5: Cultural and Creative Design: Visual Communication Design , Graduation Project: Visual Communication Design , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

Graduates may also apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes of local universities: The Hong Kong Academy for Performing Arts: BFA (Hons) in Theatre and Entertainment Arts The Hong Kong Polytechnic University: BA (Hons) in Design (Environmental Design) / BA (Hons) in Design (Interior Design) Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on an individual basis.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as designer for stage, concert, film and TV set, theatre set, props, event, exhibition, conference, museum, window display and shopping mall decoration & festival design. And, also eligible to work as project coordinator for event and activity’s planning and logistic co-ordinations.

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Civil Engineering
OfferingCampusOrVenue: IVE (Morrison Hill / Tuen Mun / Tsing Yi)
StudyPlaces: IVE (Morrison Hill) - 40 , IVE (Tuen Mun) - 60 , IVE (Tsing Yi) - 75
ProfessionalRecognition:
The programme is widely accepted by the industry. It is accredited by the Hong Kong Institution of Engineers (HKIE) and the Hong Kong Institute of Construction Managers (HKICM) as a required academic qualification for their Associate Membership. Recognition from other relevant professional bodies will also be sought.


Graduates with relevant work experience are eligible to become Grades T1 to T3 of Technically Competent Person (TCP) of specific streams and scopes under the Site Supervision Plan System of the Buildings Ordinance.


Graduates completed the required module of the programme will be issued with Green Cards which fulfills the Mandatory Basic Safety Training requirement for working in construction sites.


The programme is a CIC-accredited BIM Coordinator (CCBC) Course.

Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BEng (Hons) in Civil Engineering Graduates may apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes offered by local and overseas universities, including: The University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology The Hong Kong Polytechnic University The City University of Hong Kong RMIT University, Australia (Top-up Degree programme operated at SHAPE of VTC) University of Wales, Swansea (UK) (complete the Bachelor in Engineering (Civil) programme in two years. Higher Diploma graduates with Distinction or Credit award may be admitted directly to the Master Degree programme and complete the Master in Engineering (Civil) in two years.)
CareerProspects: The employment rate of graduates is high. Graduates may be employed in engineering consultants, main contractors and relevant government departments to take up positions as assistant engineers, project co-coordinators, project officers, works supervisors and technical officers, etc. They can become registered civil / structural engineer with relevant Bachelor Degree qualification and training.

Curriculum.Semester 2: Construction Contract and Administration , Material and Concrete Technology , Engineering Surveying , Fluid Mechanics for Civil Engineering , Soil Mechanics and Geology , Reinforced Concrete Design , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Structural Analysis , Building Information Modelling for Construction , Further Mathematics for Construction , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Construction Management , Project Implementation of BIM Technology , Geotechnical and Foundation Engineering , Steelwork Design , Elective Module (I) , Elective Module (II) , Enrichment Module A , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
Curriculum.Semester 5: Structural Detailing , Industrial Attachment , Final Year Project for Civil Engineering , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing

StudentSharing: The HD in Civil Engineering programme and professional instructors have provided me with a high-quality, effective, and highly valuable learning experience. Through these programmes, I have acquired the foundational knowledge, practical skills, and professional training necessary for my career, enabling me to obtain the qualification of a professional registered engineer and contribute to society.  After graduation, I entered Hong Kong Polytechnic University to pursue a Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, preparing myself fully to become a professional civil engineer.

The Diploma of Foundation Studies allowed me to explore engineering knowledge. I was awarded a scholarship for an overseas exchange, which broadened my horizons. After graduation, I advanced to Higher Diploma and then smoothly transitioned to Bachelor’s Degree, laying a solid foundation for my career development.

StudentSharing: Studying at IVE provided me with the theoretical and practical knowledge needed to solve engineering problems and enhance project management efficiency. The practical training in various simulated classrooms allowed me to experience real work environments firsthand. IVE also offered opportunities for overseas exchanges, enabling me to gain insights into the construction techniques and sustainability aspects of multinational corporations. This experience was tremendously valuable for my professional and personal development, broadening my horizons. After graduating with the Higher Diploma in Building Studies, I was admitted to Hong Kong Polytechnic University for degree studie. Currently, I work as an Assistant Engineer at Able Engineering Holdings Limited.

StudentSharing.Bonnie Suen: Assistant Engineer, Able Engineering Company Limited

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma in Construction Management at IVE provided me with an in-depth understanding of the construction industry. I successfully progressed to Degree in Civil Engineering at THEi, taking a solid step towards my professional goal and becoming an assistant geotechnical engineer.

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Surveying
ProfessionalRecognition: The programme has been recognised as having academic qualifications of a sufficient standard for entry to the Assessment of Professional Competence by the Hong Kong Institute of Surveyors (HKIS). Graduates with relevant working experience can apply as Probationer to undertake prescribed professional training (normally not less than 24 months) and apply for HKIS Assessment of Professional Competence to be admitted as Associate Members of HKIS. The programme is widely accepted by the construction industry. It is also accredited by the Hong Kong Institute of Construction Managers (HKICM) as a required academic qualification for their Associate Membership.Graduates with relevant work experience are eligible to become Grades T1 to T3 of Technically Competent Person (TCP) for specific streams and scopes under the Site Supervision Plan System of the Buildings Ordinance. (This applies to graduates of the BS Elective Module Set only). Graduates meet the academic requirements for the Property Management Services Authority – Property Management Practitioner (Tier 2) License. Students who complete the required module of the programme will be issued with Green Cards, which fulfill the Mandatory Basic Safety Training requirement for working on construction sites. The programme is currently a CIC-accredited BIM Coordinator (CCBC) Course.

StudentSharing: Upon completing the Higher Diploma in Surveying, I enrolled the joint part-time Bachelor's Degree programme offered by SHAPE in collaboration with UK universities. This articulation programme can be completed in as little as 2 years, allowing students to enter the workforce sooner and gain practical experience. The programmes have effectively equipped me with the professional knowledge in surveying and construction, laying a solid foundation for my future entry into the surveying industry. These programmes are well-regarded in the industry, and graduates have the opportunity to apply to various surveying consultancy firms or main contractors according to their preferences. Moreover, these programmes are recognised by local universities, offering me the chance to pursue a Master's Degree locally and potentially obtain the qualification of a registered Quantity Surveyor in the local context, as well as membership in other professional associations.

StudentSharing.Ho Man Hou: Quantity Surveyor, China Overseas Land & Investment Limited

StudentSharing: While working, I realised the importance of specialisation for career development, prompting me to return to campus and pursue the Higher Diploma at IVE Engineering. During my pursuit of the Higher Diploma, I had the opportunity to explore various aspects of engineering, which helped me better understand my interests. When it came to choosing articulation at University, teachers carefully analysed my strengths and industry characteristics, providing suitable suggestions. Ultimately, I chose to develop my career in the field of quantity surveying, gradually embarking on the path to becoming a surveyor. Furthermore, the internships arranged by IVE Engineering laid a solid foundation for my career. I recall before graduating with the Higher Diploma, I had to intern at a construction company. I was fortunate to be involved in the construction process of the M+ Museum. This internship not only broadened my horizons but also paved the way for my future career. After graduating from university, I rejoined the same company as an Assistant Quantity Surveyor.

StudentSharing.Cynthia Chong: Assistant Quantity Surveyor for a large multinational construction company
