For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BA (Hons) in Landscape Architecture Graduates may apply for admission to relevant Degree programmes offered by the local or overseas universities, including: The University of Plymouth, UK The University of South Australia, Australia The City University of Hong Kong, BSc (Hons) in Architecture The Chu Hai College of Higher Education, BSc (Hons) in Architecture Graduates may also apply for the following part-time top-up Degree programme operated by the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE) locally at VTC: BSc (Hons) in Architectural Studies # , the University of Lancashire, UK BSc (Hons) in Architectural Technology & Design # , the University of the West of England # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed in Architectural / Interior Design firms, Property Developers, Main Contractors, as Architectural Technicians, BIM Technicians, Technical Officer and Project Co-ordinators. They can pursue further study for career advancement leading to a career of Architects, BIM Managers or related professionals.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Architectural Drawing and Drafting , Architectural History A , Architectural Design A , Construction Technology and Structures , Safety and Industrial Training , Building Services for Architecture , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Design
Curriculum.Semester 2: Architectural History B , Computer Aided Drawing and Modelling , Architectural Materials and Detailing A , Architectural Design B , Architectural Practice A , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Environment and Sustainable Design , Building Information Modelling for Construction , Architectural Design C , Enrichment Module B , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search
Curriculum.Semester 4: Enrichment Module A , Project Implementation of BIM Technology , Final Year Architectural Project , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Elective Module (select 2 out of the 3 modules: Architectural Practice B, Interior Architectural Studies and Advanced BIM Skills)
Curriculum.Semester 5: Architectural Materials and Detailing B , Industrial Attachment , Final Year Architectural Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing

StudentSharing: I began with IVE's Diploma of Foundation Studies, advanced to a Higher Diploma, and later earned a Bachelor's Degree at THEi. During my studies, an internship with an engineering consultancy provided me with valuable industry insights and practice for paving my way to a professional engineer.

StudentSharing.Myra Tsang: Assistant Building Services Engineer, Gammon Construction Limited

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma programme at IVE combines theory and practice, covering a broad spectrum of areas. Through exchange activities and internships, I gained a deep understanding of the industry’s practical operations. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to study at a UK university, where I pursued a Bachelor’s and Doctoral Degree. I am now a chartered engineer, contributing to green engineering development at a major energy company in the UK.

StudentSharing.Bobo Ng: Associate Director of Decarbonisation, AECOM Non Executive Director of the UK Power Corporation

StudentSharing: During my time at IVE, I participated in different field studies, which allowed me to integrate classroom learning with real-world work environments, making my studies more effective. After graduating from Higher Diploma, I was awarded a scholarship to study for a Bachelor’s Degree in Construction Management and Surveying in the UK. I later completed a Master’s Degree, realising my dream of becoming a surveyor.

The Higher Diploma in Construction Management at IVE provided me with an in-depth understanding of the construction industry. I successfully progressed to Degree in Civil Engineering at THEi, taking a solid step towards my professional goal and becoming an assistant geotechnical engineer.

The IVE Higher Diploma allowed me to accumulate extensive practical experiences. I participated in the World Solar Challenge for three consecutive years, designing the power systems and energy management programmes for solar-powered vehicles, and won third place in the Australian competition. These valuable experiences enhanced my competitiveness and greatly supported my progression to university.

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Environmental Engineering
ProfessionalRecognition:
The programme is widely accepted by the industry. It is accredited by the Hong Kong Institution of Engineers as a required academic qualification for their Associate Membership. Recognition from other relevant professional bodies will also be sought.


Graduates completed the required module of the programme will be issued with Green Cards which fulfills the Mandatory Basic Safety Training requirement for working in construction sites.


Graduates with relevant work experience are eligible to apply for exemption for some modules of the Professional Certificate in Environmental Management for Construction (Environmental Officer). The Professional Certificate is recognised by the Development Bureau as a qualification that satisfies the requirement of the Environmental Officer Course as stated in Appendix C of ETWB Technical Circular (Works) No. 19/2005. With Vplus Engineering under the Vplus Subsidy Scheme, eligible students may apply up to 60% reimbursement of their tuition fee.

Curriculum.Semester 3: Basic Mathematics for Environmental Studies (Part 2) , Air and Noise Control Engineering (Part 2) , Sustainability and Engineering Profession , Building Services for Environmental EngineeringProfessional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , IT Essentials - Smart Living , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module A

Curriculum.Semester 5: Quality and OSH Management , Final Year Project (Part 2) , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation

StudentSharing: During my time studying for the Higher Diploma at IVE, I had the privilege of participating in exchange activities in Japan, where I discussed the development of renewable energy technologies with local university students. Additionally, I had the opportunity to intern at a local engineering company, focusing on improving the quality of seawater. These enriching learning experiences inspired me to further my studies after completing the Higher Diploma, leading me to pursue a Bachelor's Degree in Civil and Environmental Engineering at a local university, followed by obtaining a Master's Degree. Currently, I hold the position of Assistant Environmental Protection Officer at the Environmental Protection Department, where I am able to showcase my expertise and talents in the field of environmental engineering.

StudentSharing.Chan Ho Hoi, Nikita: Assistant Environmental Protection Officer, Environmental Protection Department

StudentSharing: During my studies for the Higher Diploma at IVE, I was introduced to the architecture industry for the first time and gained a profound understanding of the importance of Building Information Modeling (BIM) in construction projects. After graduation, armed with the knowledge acquired at IVE, I successfully joined China State Construction International Holdings Limited. During this period, I continued my studies in the articulation Bachelor's Degree programme provided by VTC SHAPE and obtained my Degree.  After that, I earned a Master's Degree at the University of Hong Kong, graduating with distinction in 2024. The courses at IVE have truly been a guiding light in my career, illuminating the path for my future endeavors.

StudentSharing.Cai Jiada: BIM-related position, China State Construction International Holdings Limited

StudentSharing: The IVE Engineering Higher Diploma covers various engineering areas and provides opportunities to engage with new technologies such as BIM, helping me grasp the digitalisation trend in the engineering industry. I have worked with a team using 4D BIM models to simulate construction scenarios for railway projects, adding a time axis to the existing 3D drawings to understand the construction process better and accelerate construction efficiency. BIM serves as a common language for all participants in engineering projects, enhancing design and construction efficiency, approval speed, and post-delivery maintenance management. There is a strong demand in the industry for engineering professionals who can master the application of new technologies.

StudentSharing.Monika Lau: Regional Information Manager (Asia), multinational engineering consulting company

ProfessionalRecognition: IVE (Haking Wong) offers a stream in Electrical Services in cooperation with Shenzhen Polytechnic University. 1

VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies with successful completion of Foundation Mathematics III; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education with successful completion of Mathematics 3G / 3E; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate with Grade C or above in IGCSE Mathematics; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education with successful completion of Mathematics Plus; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Holders of VTC Foundation Diploma (Level 3) / Diploma of Foundation Studies / VTC Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education / Yi Jin Diploma / Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education without successful completion of the relevant optional / elective Mathematics module, should possess HKDSE Mathematics / Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) at Level 2 or above.

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BEng (Hons) in Building Services Engineering BSc (Hons) in Green Engineering and Sustainability Graduates may apply for admission to the relevant BEng (Hons) programmes offered by the local universities. Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on individual basis. Higher Diploma is recognised by most overseas universities. Special offers are given by the RMIT University, Australia (2-year full-time and 8-semester part-time BEng programmes studying in HK in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE) of VTC).
CareerProspects: Graduates are very much welcomed by the industry. They are normally employed as assistant engineers, technicians, supervisors, technical sales engineers. Most of them work in the areas of electrical services, electrical installation, power engineering & smart grid, E&M operation and maintenance, electrical & electronic servicing, intelligent systems, energy audit and management, lighting systems.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Engineering Mathematics , Circuit Theory , Electronic Devices , Computer-Aided Drawing , Practical Training A , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Digital Electronics and Microprocessor Fundamentals , Electrical Instrumentation and Measurement , Electrical Engineering Principles , OSH and Sustainable Environmental Practices , Practical Training B , Programming Technique Fundamentals for Automation and Control System , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 3: Electrical Installation , Electrical Power Systems , Electrical Machines and Drives , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search
Curriculum.Semester 4: Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Control and Automation Systems , Enrichment Module A , Elective Module 1* , Elective Module 2*
Curriculum.Semester 5: Engineering Project Management and Supervisory Skills , Industrial Attachment , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing
Curriculum.*Elective Modules: Electrical Services in Building , Energy Efficiency and Management , Intelligent Control , Lighting Technology and System Design , Entertainment Lighting Control System , Advanced Engineering Mathematics , Railway System , Smart Grid and Digital Power Industry , BIM-MEP
Curriculum.Electrical Services Stream Specific Modu: PLC and Industrial Control , Electrical Engineering Project , Electric Machine and Drive , New Energy Technology , Electrical Distribution Technology , BIM for Electrical and Mechanical Engineering Systems , Integrated Applications in Artificial Intelligence and Robotic System , Major Project

StudentSharing: After studying the applied learning programme in electrical and energy engineering in secondary school, I aspired to pursue a career in the energy sector and worked diligently to build my expertise. Upon graduation, I enrolled in the Higher Diploma in Electrical Engineering at IVE, where the school provided abundant learning and internship opportunities that broadened my horizons. For instance, I joined the VTC Solar Car Team Sophie, where I handled external relations, and participated in the opening ceremony of CLP Power Engineering Laboratory, presenting my research on smart meters to industry experts. These valuable experiences not only enhanced my soft skills but also deepened my industry knowledge. With the support and assistance of teachers and classmates, I had the privilege to participate in various competitions and receive awards, enriching my learning journey. I am deeply grateful for the generous scholarship support provided by IVE, which has allowed me to continue my education in the field of electrical engineering and even pursue further studies in the UK. I look forward to entering the field of smart grids after completing my Bachelor's Degree, contributing my efforts towards achieving a greener future.

StudentSharing.Kitty Chan: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Electrical Engineering, IVE

StudentSharing: The IVE Higher Diploma allowed me to accumulate extensive practical experiences. I participated in the World Solar Challenge for three consecutive years, designing the power systems and energy management programmes for solar-powered vehicles, and won third place in the Australian competition. These valuable experiences enhanced my competitiveness and greatly supported my progression to university.

While studying for my Higher Diploma at IVE, I had the privilege of participating in the development of the solar-powered vehicle SOPHIE 8 and joined an international competition in Australia. This valuable experience inspired me to embark on an entrepreneurial journey, developing solar energy converters. Today, in addition to continuing my research in the field of renewable energy, I have also begun exploring STEAM education, hoping to integrate technical knowledge with education to drive further innovation.

StudentSharing.Joe Lee: Co-founder & CEO, SOWER Engineering Limited and Global Pacific Group (Hong Kong) Limited

StudentSharing: Through studying subjects like Computer Design and Embedded Software Development, I acquired the technical skills to design computer boards and develop applications. During my internship, I gained recognition from my superiors and was subsequently offered long-term employment. Continuing my education, I completed a Bachelor's Degree at The Chinese University of Hong Kong and a Master's Degree at The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology. During my third year at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, I ventured into entrepreneurship, a journey I continue to pursue. The company I founded specialises in developing smart systems for personal accessories, such as luggage displaying weight and wallets featuring anti-theft functions.

StudentSharing: I began with IVE's Diploma of Foundation Studies, advanced to a Higher Diploma, and later earned a Bachelor's Degree at THEi. During my studies, an internship with an engineering consultancy provided me with valuable industry insights and practice for paving my way to a professional engineer.

StudentSharing.Myra Tsang: Assistant Building Services Engineer, Gammon Construction Limited

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma programme at IVE combines theory and practice, covering a broad spectrum of areas. Through exchange activities and internships, I gained a deep understanding of the industry’s practical operations. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to study at a UK university, where I pursued a Bachelor’s and Doctoral Degree. I am now a chartered engineer, contributing to green engineering development at a major energy company in the UK.

StudentSharing.Bobo Ng: Associate Director of Decarbonisation, AECOM Non Executive Director of the UK Power Corporation

StudentSharing: During my time at IVE, I participated in different field studies, which allowed me to integrate classroom learning with real-world work environments, making my studies more effective. After graduating from Higher Diploma, I was awarded a scholarship to study for a Bachelor’s Degree in Construction Management and Surveying in the UK. I later completed a Master’s Degree, realising my dream of becoming a surveyor.

The Higher Diploma in Construction Management at IVE provided me with an in-depth understanding of the construction industry. I successfully progressed to Degree in Civil Engineering at THEi, taking a solid step towards my professional goal and becoming an assistant geotechnical engineer.

The IVE Higher Diploma allowed me to accumulate extensive practical experiences. I participated in the World Solar Challenge for three consecutive years, designing the power systems and energy management programmes for solar-powered vehicles, and won third place in the Australian competition. These valuable experiences enhanced my competitiveness and greatly supported my progression to university.

StudentSharing: With the recommendation of an IVE Engineering teacher, I participated in the WorldSkills Competition, known as the Skills Olympics. After excelling in the electromechanical integration competition at the Hong Kong regional selection, we became the representatives of Hong Kong and competed in the 2019 WorldSkills Competition held in Kazan, Russia, ultimately winning an Medallion for Excellence. During the training period, I am grateful for the unlimited support provided by the IVE teacher, which gave us the opportunity to interact with competitors from around the world. This experience enriched my life experiences and made me deeply appreciate this valuable learning opportunity.

StudentSharing.Au-yeung Ka Ming: Graduate, Higher Diploma of Mechanical Engineering, IVE

Graduates may apply for the following HKCAAVQ accredited full-time / part-time top-up Degree programmes [duration of 9 months (full-time) / 18 months (part-time)] jointly operated by the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE) of VTC: BEng (Hons) in Building Services and Sustainable Engineering # offered by the University of Lancashire, UK (Part-time); Registration Number (Non-Local Higher and Professional Education (Regulation) Ordinance) : 252295 BEng (Hons) in Building Services Engineering # offered by the University of the West of England, UK (Full-time and part-time); Registration Number (Non-Local Higher and Professional Education (Regulation) Ordinance) : 252610 # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed in various areas of infrastructure development and construction related projects including new developments, urban renewal projects, maintenance and upgrading of existing services. Nature of work varies from building services design and installation to facility management. The multi-skill training offered by the programme renders graduates less vulnerable to fluctuations in the construction industry. The average employment rate in recent years is over 90%. Graduates may be employed in both private and public organisations, e.g. building services consultants and contractors, property development and management companies, main contractors, suppliers and government departments. A student placement programme is built in the programme with students being placed as far as possible to work full-time in industry during the last semester of study. This helps to achieve the objective of "seamless employment".

Curriculum.Semester 1: Electrical Services Fundamentals , Mechanical Services Fundamentals , Engineering Mathematics for Building Services , Building Services Drawing , Engineering Safety and Environmental Awareness , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , IT Essentials - Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Electrical Installations , HVACR Technology , Piped and Fire Services , Building Information Modelling for MEP Coordination , Practical Training for Building Services , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports
Curriculum.Semester 3: Electrical Machines , Testing and Commissioning , Piped and Fire Services Design , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Sustainable Built Environment and HVACR Systems , Mechanical and Electrical Services Design , Advanced Piped and Fire Services , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement , Enrichment Module A , Elective Module
Curriculum.Semester 5: Construction Technology and Management , Industrial Attachment , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

StudentSharing: While knowledge and working experience are important, but the critical element is actually building your foundation in an early stage of tertiary education so you can develop the career for life. IVE Engineering is definitely an enabler for me to devote in the engineering field that I aspire.

I began with IVE's Diploma of Foundation Studies, advanced to a Higher Diploma, and later earned a Bachelor's Degree at THEi. During my studies, an internship with an engineering consultancy provided me with valuable industry insights and practice for paving my way to a professional engineer.

StudentSharing.Myra Tsang: Assistant Building Services Engineer, Gammon Construction Limited

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Aircraft Maintenance Engineering
ProfessionalRecognition: The programme is approved by the Civil Aviation Department of the HKSAR as an approved aircraft maintenance training course. The programme has been accredited by the Hong Kong Institution of Engineers as satisfying the academic requirements for its associate membership. Partial Tuition Refund Scheme for the Specialised Aircraft Maintenance Programme: Graduates from the programme meeting the requirements are eligible to apply for the scheme. Successful applicants may receive 50% refund of the total tuition fee paid for the Higher Diploma in Aircraft Maintenance Engineering, subject to a maximum sum of HK$50,000 (whichever is the less) each. For more details, please refer to: https://www.tlb.gov.hk/matf/eng/aviation/partial-refund.html
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies with successful completion of Foundation Mathematics III; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education with successful completion of Mathematics 3G / 3E; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate with Grade C or above in IGCSE Mathematics; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education with successful completion of Mathematics Plus; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Holders of VTC Foundation Diploma (Level 3) / Diploma of Foundation Studies / VTC Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education / Yi Jin Diploma / Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education without successful completion of the relevant optional / elective Mathematics module, should possess HKDSE Mathematics / Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) at Level 2 or above.

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BEng (Hons) in Aircraft Engineering Graduates may apply for admission to the relevant BEng (Hons) programmes offered by the following local universities: The University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology The Chinese University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Polytechnic University The City University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Metropolitan University The Hong Kong Baptist University The Education University of Hong Kong Graduates may apply for admission to the top-up BAppSc in Aviation jointly run in Hong Kong by School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE) of VTC and the RMIT University, Australia. Please refer to the corresponding programme page on the website of SHAPE . Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on individual basis. Higher Diploma is recognised by most overseas universities. Special offers are given by: The University of Sheffield, UK The Swansea University, Wales, UK The University of Liverpool, UK
CareerProspects: The programme is designed in accordance with the Hong Kong Aviation Requirements-66 so as to train up students to a standard recognised by the authorities and the industry in the field of aircraft maintenance engineering. Graduates may be employed by aircraft maintenance companies, airline companies, airfreight cargo operators and/or related organisations / companies in the aviation industry. Graduates may apply for full-time or part-time related professional Degree programmes at local or overseas universities. The Hong Kong Aircraft Engineering Co. Ltd. (HAECO) and IVE(TY) collaborate to offer an Industrial Training programme. Students being successfully selected may undergo training at HAECO.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Fundamental Engineering Science , Mathematics, Physics and Aircraft Aerodynamics , Engineering Practical Training , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Aircraft Electrical & Electronic Fundamentals , Materials & Hardware (A) , Materials & Hardware (B) , Aircraft Maintenance Practices (A) , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
Curriculum.Semester 3: Digital Techniques and Electronic Instrument Systems , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Aircraft Maintenance Practices B (B1.1)
Curriculum.Semester 4: Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation & Reports , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement , Enrichment Module A , Gas Turbine Engine (A) , Gas Turbine Engine (B) , Propeller , Aeroplane Structures
Curriculum.Semester 5: Aeroplane Aerodynamics, Structures & Systems (I) , Aviation Legislation, Human Factors & Project Management , Aircraft Practical Training and Actual Maintenance Practices , Avionic Practical Training and Actual Maintenance Practices
Curriculum.Semester 6: Aeroplane Aerodynamics, Structures & Systems (II) , Airframe Systems , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Enrichment Module B

StudentSharing: The studies at IVE Engineering have been a memorable and life-changing journey for me. The programme provided ample practical training opportunities, an excellent teaching team, and courses tailored to meet market demands, equipping me with the necessary skills and professional knowledge to advance my career in the aviation industry. This has further enabled me to adequately prepare for the professional aircraft maintenance qualification exam, helping me obtain the Civil Aviation Department of Hong Kong SAR Government recognised HKAR-66 Aircraft Maintenance License.

StudentSharing: Studying at IVE has equipped me well on my learning journey. In comparison to traditional classroom learning methods, the teachers at IVE Engineering often encourage us to learn through different avenues, such as participating in various engineering competitions and making full use of the advanced teaching equipment on campus to conduct multiple experiments to gain practical experience. In addition to classroom learning, I have also been awarded multiple scholarships from IVE Engineering, which have helped me to equip myself fully and continue my education and develop my future career.

StudentSharing.Jack Lau: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Automotive Engineering, IVE

CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed in the merchant navy as deck cadets. They may also find their job opportunities in ship management / agency companies, ferry companies, stevedoring / sea freight agents, dockyards, warehousing / container terminals, fuel / inventory supply companies, marine consultancy companies, Marine Department or disciplinary forces of HKSAR, etc. Subject to the continuing implementation of ^^ Sea-going Training Incentive Scheme by the Government, graduates undergoing their cadet training aboard ocean-going vessels are eligible to receive training incentives from the Government. For details, please visit the website of the Marine Department of HKSAR ( https://www.mardep.gov.hk ). ^^ Only applicable to permanent Hong Kong resident

Curriculum.Semester 1: Fundamentals of Mathematics for Maritime Studies , Occupational Training (B) , General Ship Knowledge (A) , Ocean Navigation (A) , Electronic Navigation Systems (A) , Cargo Work (A) , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Occupational Training (A) , General Ship Knowledge (B) , Ocean Navigation (B) , Electronic Navigation Systems (B) , Navigational Watch-keeping , Marine Meteorology , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part A)
Curriculum.Semester 3: Elementary First Aid and Proficiency in Medical First Aid (Combined) Certificate , Security Training for Seafarers with Designated Security Duties , Personal Safety and Social Responsibilities , Personal Survival Techniques , Fire Prevention, Fire Fighting and Advanced Fire Fighting (Combined) , Proficiency in Survival Craft and Rescue Boats other than Fast Rescue Boats , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part B)
Curriculum.Semester 4: Occupational Training (C) Part A , Chartwork and Coastal Navigation , Bridge Resource Management , Ocean Navigation (C) , Ship Safety / Security and Emergency Responses , Final Year Project (Phase 1) , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 5: Occupational Training (C) (Part B) , Ship Construction and Maintenance , Ship Stability (A) , Cargo Work (B) , Passage Planning (A) , Final Year Project (Phase 2) , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Enrichment Module B

StudentSharing: IVE Engineering has provided me with essential core knowledge and professional skills for my career development. With the help of a range of new equipment and systematic practical training, I have been able to adapt more easily to maritime life. Moreover, the instructors at MSTI have rich experience in the maritime industry, they inspire and guide students by sharing their own stories and insights. MSTI also offers career counseling, helping us explore various development opportunities in the industry and providing professional advice when needed. Currently, I am working as a deck intern at a shipping company, and I still keep in touch with IVE Engineering to stay updated on the latest developments in the maritime industry.

StudentSharing: I began with IVE's Diploma of Foundation Studies, advanced to a Higher Diploma, and later earned a Bachelor's Degree at THEi. During my studies, an internship with an engineering consultancy provided me with valuable industry insights and practice for paving my way to a professional engineer.

StudentSharing.Myra Tsang: Assistant Building Services Engineer, Gammon Construction Limited

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma programme at IVE combines theory and practice, covering a broad spectrum of areas. Through exchange activities and internships, I gained a deep understanding of the industry’s practical operations. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to study at a UK university, where I pursued a Bachelor’s and Doctoral Degree. I am now a chartered engineer, contributing to green engineering development at a major energy company in the UK.

StudentSharing.Bobo Ng: Associate Director of Decarbonisation, AECOM Non Executive Director of the UK Power Corporation

StudentSharing: During my time at IVE, I participated in different field studies, which allowed me to integrate classroom learning with real-world work environments, making my studies more effective. After graduating from Higher Diploma, I was awarded a scholarship to study for a Bachelor’s Degree in Construction Management and Surveying in the UK. I later completed a Master’s Degree, realising my dream of becoming a surveyor.

The Higher Diploma in Construction Management at IVE provided me with an in-depth understanding of the construction industry. I successfully progressed to Degree in Civil Engineering at THEi, taking a solid step towards my professional goal and becoming an assistant geotechnical engineer.

The IVE Higher Diploma allowed me to accumulate extensive practical experiences. I participated in the World Solar Challenge for three consecutive years, designing the power systems and energy management programmes for solar-powered vehicles, and won third place in the Australian competition. These valuable experiences enhanced my competitiveness and greatly supported my progression to university.

Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to the relevant BEng (Hons) Degree programmes offered by the local universities. Upon admission to relevant Degree programmes, graduates may apply for credit exemption. The level of exemption will be considered on individual basis by respective universities. The University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology The Chinese University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Polytechnic University The City University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Metropolitan University The Hong Kong Baptist University The Education University of Hong Kong Graduates may apply for admission to the top-up BASc in Aviation # jointly run in Hong Kong by School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE) of VTC and the RMIT University, Australia. Higher Diploma in Aviation Systems and Operations is recognised by RMIT University, Australia. Graduates with theories on the pilot licence in the programme can opt to further their pilot training and flying examinations in Australia for the pilot licence and the top-up BASc in Aviation. # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: For graduates of the Higher Diploma in Aviation, there is a broad range of career opportunities in the areas of air transportation, ground operations, logistics, airlines and airport operation, maintenance and repair, and government departments. Graduates may be employed as operation engineer, logistics engineer, planning officer, technical officer, fleet controller, cadet pilot, etc.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Introduction to Aviation , Introduction to Aircraft , Fundamental of Mathematics for Aviation , Aviation Mathematics , Airport and Airline Operations , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Smart Living
Curriculum.Semester 2: Aircraft Systems , Managing Engineering Environment , Aviation Industry Environment , Industrial Automation and Control , Airline Operations , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Introduction to Air Cargo Operations , Ramp and Ground Handling Operations
Curriculum.Semester 4: Human Factors in Aviation , Aviation Project Management , Aviation Safety, Security and Business Continuity , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module B

StudentSharing: Compared to other tertiary institutions, I believe that IVE Engineering not only provides professional theoretical courses but also emphasises students' practical experience in the industry. The curriculum includes arranging internships for students, giving me the opportunity to participate in daily airport operations and acquire relevant practical skills, which has made me more responsible and clarified my future career direction. I am very grateful for this internship opportunity, which has equipped me with the necessary workplace skills. Furthermore, IVE Engineering also offers scholarships for overseas studies. I am fortunate to have received the Hong Kong Jockey Club Scholarship, which supports me in pursuing my dream of flying! The IVE programme has provided me with the knowledge and skills to enter university, and has laid the foundation for my current role as a trainee pilot and future career as an airline pilot. Therefore, I will seize every opportunity to give back to my juniors and encourage them to set goals for dreams at IVE Engineering. In conclusion, IVE Engineering has facilitated my continuous growth, and I believe that the knowledge and experience gained will have a positive impact on my future career development. Choosing IVE Engineering has been a wise decision!

When I was a child, I always dreamed of becoming a pilot. After finishing secondary school, I joined the IVE Higher Diploma in Aviation (Currently named as Aviation Systems and Operations) programme. During my studies at IVE, It has allowed me to gain extensive knowledge about the development and management of the aviation industry. IVE also offered a wide ranges of courses, including simulated learning and training at the Simulated Airport Operations Centre. This experience led to a valuable internship opportunity at the Airport Authority, where I gained a better understanding of industry operations.  With the support of the Scholarship, I was able to pursue further studies in the UK and obtain a Bachelor’s degree. Currently, I am working towards a Master's degree in the UK, and hoping to make a meaningful contribution to the aviation industry after graduation.

StudentSharing.Victor Lam: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Aviation, IVE (renamed as Higher Diploma in Aviation System and Operations)

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

StudentSharing: I initially aimed to pursue advanced studies while studying at IVE, with no expectations of studying in the UK. However, the teachers at IVE provided dedicated guidance and opened up new opportunities for students, enabling me to fulfill my dream of studying abroad. Ultimately, I completed my bachelor’s degree in the UK and achieved more than I had ever anticipated.

StudentSharing.Adelaide Lo: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Environmental Science, IVE

StudentSharing: While studying the Higher Diploma programme at IVE, I interned at an accredited medical laboratory and an ophthalmology center. These experiences enhanced my understanding of the practical duties of a medical laboratory technologist and the day-to-day operations within a lab. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to complete my Bachelor's and Master's Degrees in the UK, laying the foundation for obtaining professional qualifications.

I started with the Higher Diploma in Health and Life Sciences at IVE, successfully progressing to a Bachelor’s Degree at Coventry University in the UK, followed by a Master of Business Administration at City University of Hong Kong. The IVE programme helped me advance step by step towards professionalism, and after graduation, I entered the environmental engineering industry.

StudentSharing.Kenny Ling: Chief Sustainability Officer, International EHS Enterprise

StudentSharing: I have always been interested in biotechnology, so I enrolled in related courses at IVE. From there, I gained valuable industry internship opportunities, participated in genetic research, and applied classroom knowledge. Subsequently, I was awarded a scholarship from the Alistair Harvey Foundation Scholarship to pursue bachelor's and master's degrees in the UK, strengthening the foundation for my professional development. Now, I work at the National Cancer Center in Singapore, specializing in cancer genetics, and I am grateful to IVE for helping me embark on the path of scientific research!

StudentSharing.Dr. Shuen Wai Ho Timothy: "Research Manager and Senior Research Fellow, National Cancer Centre Singapore (NCCS) "

StudentSharing: I have always had a strong interest in biological research. Curriculum.Semester 2: Tree Biology and Physiology , Site and Vegetation Survey , Tree Planting and Tree Survey , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence+ , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement+ , IT Essentials - health-and-life-sciences+ , Enrichment Module B

Curriculum.Semester 4: Diagnosis and Plant Disorders , Tree Pruning Practice , Conservation Biology , Tree Climbing Practice , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation+ , Project
Curriculum.Semester 5: Tree Risk Assessment , Tree Preservation Management , Urban Forest and Conservation , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search+ , Project

StudentSharing: While studying the Higher Diploma programme at IVE, I interned at an accredited medical laboratory and an ophthalmology center. These experiences enhanced my understanding of the practical duties of a medical laboratory technologist and the day-to-day operations within a lab. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to complete my Bachelor's and Master's Degrees in the UK, laying the foundation for obtaining professional qualifications.

I started with the Higher Diploma in Health and Life Sciences at IVE, successfully progressing to a Bachelor’s Degree at Coventry University in the UK, followed by a Master of Business Administration at City University of Hong Kong. The IVE programme helped me advance step by step towards professionalism, and after graduation, I entered the environmental engineering industry.

StudentSharing.Kenny Ling: Chief Sustainability Officer, International EHS Enterprise

StudentSharing: After completing the Higher Diploma of Biomedical Sciences, I gained a deeper understanding of the biomedical industry. I then progressed to a Bachelor’s Degree. Currently, I am working as an assistant medical laboratory technologist at a public hospital, applying my professional knowledge to excel in my role.

StudentSharing.Wong Pui Hang: Associate Medical Technologist at a public hospital

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Occupational Safety and Emergency Operations
ProfessionalRecognition: The programme is recognised by the Labour Department of the HKSAR as a Scheduled Qualification for registration as Safety Officer under the Factories and Industrial Undertakings (Safety Officers and Safety Supervisors) Regulations. Graduates possess relevant work experience are eligible to apply for Registered Safety Officer (RSO).
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to the following programmes : BSc (Hons) in Environmental and Occupational Safety and Health offered by the Hong Kong Polytechnic University; BSc (Hons) in Environmental Science offered by Plymouth University, United Kingdom; BSc (Hons) in Environmental Management and Sustainability offered by the Plymouth University, United Kingdom; BSc (Hons) in Occupational Safety and Health Management offered by Middlesex University London, United Kingdom; and BSc (Hons) in Occupational Safety and Health offered by University of the West of Scotland, United Kingdom.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as assistant safety officer or inspector in government departments (Labour Department, Housing Authority, etc.), public utilities, professional safety consultants firms, construction project management companies. Other opportunities include career development in emergency operations, such as ambulanceman in health services providers and risk control assistant, crisis management assistant in event management companies or hotels.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Essential Chemistry & Applications , Safety Technology , Life Science for Occupational Safety and Health , Health Promotion & Safety Training , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , IT Essentials - health-and-life-sciences , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness
Curriculum.Semester 2: Ergonomics , Safety and Health Legislation , Workplace Safety I , Risk Assessment and Inspection , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part A)
Curriculum.Semester 3: Statistics and Social Survey Techniques for Safety and Health , Computer Aided Drawing , Industrial Attachment , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part B) , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Fundamental of Occupational Hygiene , Workplace Safety II , Occupational Health & Toxicology , Safety and Health Management , Project , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 5: Quality & System Reliability , Occupational Hygiene Assessment , Risk Management & Modelling , Audit & Accident Investigation , Project , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma in Pet Care and Wellness is an incredible stepping stone for my future in the pet industry! What really stands out for me are the exciting extracurricular visits to animal welfare organizations and veterinary hospitals. These experiences, along with the diverse and hands-on workshops, have truly ignited my passion and deepened my understanding of this field. I've gained a wealth of knowledge about pets—ranging from grooming to health care and training. Each lesson has been an adventure, and I can’t wait to begin my career after graduation, armed with all these amazing experiences!

StudentSharing.Chloe Lo: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Pet Care and Wellness, IVE

I chose further study and started career at the same time, I also completed Master in Chinese Medicine of Hong Kong Baptist University. An opportunity of understanding the operation about Chinese Medicine Hospital in Mainland is given to me so I can broaden my horizons.

StudentSharing.Nicole Tsang: Senior Chinese Medicine Dispenser and Deputy-in-charge of CM Pharmacy, Yan Chai Hospital-Hong Kong Baptist University Chinese Medicine Clinic cum Training and Research Centre (Tsuen Wan District)

StudentSharing: IVE has given me the opportunity to pursue my dream of going to university. The focus here on practical and applied learning has rekindled my love for studying. During my study, I’ve gained diverse and practical rehabilitation knowledge and skills through classes, internships, and various volunteer activities. I’ve been able to apply what I’ve learned to help my family and friends in their recovery journeys.

The two-year Higher Diploma program has allowed me to explore my interests and accumulate experience through engaging teaching and diverse practical activities. Various volunteer opportunities and further education have enhanced my professional knowledge, laying a solid foundation for achieving my goals.

StudentSharing.Fung Sze Wai: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Rehabilitation Services, IVE

StudentSharing: While studying the Higher Diploma programme at IVE, I interned at an accredited medical laboratory and an ophthalmology center. These experiences enhanced my understanding of the practical duties of a medical laboratory technologist and the day-to-day operations within a lab. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to complete my Bachelor's and Master's Degrees in the UK, laying the foundation for obtaining professional qualifications.

I started with the Higher Diploma in Health and Life Sciences at IVE, successfully progressing to a Bachelor’s Degree at Coventry University in the UK, followed by a Master of Business Administration at City University of Hong Kong. The IVE programme helped me advance step by step towards professionalism, and after graduation, I entered the environmental engineering industry.

StudentSharing.Kenny Ling: Chief Sustainability Officer, International EHS Enterprise

Curriculum.Semester 5: Nutrition Therapy , Sports Science and Nutrition , Health Food Products and Their Development , Project

StudentSharing: The programme has a comprehensive coverage in nutritional studies, food safety and microbiology which allows me to show my full potential in my career and academics.

The knowledge of nutrition and food safety taught in the course has greatly increased my interest in the field of nutrition, allowing me to apply what I've learned effectively in my work. The instructors' engaging teaching style, which goes beyond textbook knowledge, has made the course content much easier to absorb.

StudentSharing.Ng Wing Wai: Assistant Field Sales Manager, Nestle Hong Kong Ltd.

These skills have made it easier for me to excel in my Enrolled Nurse program. Now that I have graduated, I hope to bring more help to the general public.

I sincerely thank all the teachers at the institute for their guidance and care, leading me through the boundless sea of knowledge and laying a solid foundation for my future studies. I will apply what I have learned to care for patients in the future.

StudentSharing.Yeung Mei Yee, Zoe: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Medical Centre Operations, IVE

CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed in various food and nutritional related industries and public health services, including food production, food product development, catering organisations, global food logistics and business trade, food product testing, health inspectors for Food and Environmental Hygiene Department and food safety auditors. Graduates are competent to fill positions at technical, supervisory and managerial levels as food technical assistants, food quality controllers, food logistic and hygiene managers, and other relevant technical practitioners in the food trade discipline.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Essential Chemistry and Applications , Statistics , Basic Microbiology , Safety and Laboratory Practice , Introduction to Food Industry and Practices , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Health & Life Sciences
Curriculum.Semester 2: Food Microbiology , Food and Nutritional Chemistry , Sensory Evaluation of Food , Human Nutrition , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part A)
Curriculum.Semester 3: Food Analysis , Food Legislation , Industrial attachment , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part B) , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 4: Food Technology , Food Processing , Food Business and Marketing , Epidemiology of Food Related Illnesses , Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 5: Food Packaging and Food Communication , Food Quality and Certification , Food Toxicology , Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

StudentSharing: While studying the Higher Diploma programme at IVE, I interned at an accredited medical laboratory and an ophthalmology center. These experiences enhanced my understanding of the practical duties of a medical laboratory technologist and the day-to-day operations within a lab. After graduation, I was awarded a scholarship to complete my Bachelor's and Master's Degrees in the UK, laying the foundation for obtaining professional qualifications.

I started with the Higher Diploma in Health and Life Sciences at IVE, successfully progressing to a Bachelor’s Degree at Coventry University in the UK, followed by a Master of Business Administration at City University of Hong Kong. The IVE programme helped me advance step by step towards professionalism, and after graduation, I entered the environmental engineering industry.

StudentSharing.Kenny Ling: Chief Sustainability Officer, International EHS Enterprise

StudentSharing: After completing the Higher Diploma of Biomedical Sciences, I gained a deeper understanding of the biomedical industry. I then progressed to a Bachelor’s Degree. Currently, I am working as an assistant medical laboratory technologist at a public hospital, applying my professional knowledge to excel in my role.

StudentSharing.Wong Pui Hang: Associate Medical Technologist at a public hospital

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in Dispensing Studies
ProfessionalRecognition: Graduates may be employed as dispensers in Hospital Authority hospitals, private hospitals, other healthcare institutions and residential care home etc.
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to the following programme: BPharm offered by Queensland University of Technology, Australia
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as dispensers in Hospital Authority hospitals, private hospitals, other healthcare institutions and residential care home etc.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Introduction to Professional Practices in Pharmaceutical Services , Essential Chemistry and Applications , Pharmaceutical Chemistry , Human Physiology , Basic Pharmacology , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Health-and-Life-Sciences
Curriculum.Semester 2: Pharmaceutics , Human Disease , System Pharmacology I , Introduction to Dispensing , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentation, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part A) , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 3: Dispensing Practice I , Industrial Attachment , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part B)
Curriculum.Semester 4: Pharmaceutical Microbiology and Infection Control , System Pharmacology II , Dispensing Practice II , Hospital Pharmacy Practice , Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 5: Patient counseling and Education , Dispensing Practice III , Drug Therapeutics , Pharmaceutical Services in Residential Care Home , Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

StudentSharing: The knowledge and practices I obtained through this programme’s curriculum was relevant to the hospital setting, which was very important for my career development. I also attended extracurricular voluntary activities organized by the school, which further enhanced my communication and interpersonal skills. All these valuable experiences sowed the seeds of becoming a well-rounded senior dispenser. This career advancement has allowed me to provide more appropriate and personalized services to patients.

StudentSharing.Tsui Tsz Tung: Senior Dispenser, Alice Ho Miu Ling Nethersole Hospital

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma in Dispensing Studies programme offers an outstanding learning environment, with the simulated dispensing and aseptic laboratories being the most memorable parts of my experience. The simulated dispensing laboratory allowed me to utilize my knowledge in real-world case scenarios, while the aseptic laboratory taught me to work accurately under pressure and time constraints. These experiences have equipped me with the skills needed to adapt to challenging pharmacy environments as a dispenser.

StudentSharing.Li Chun Fai: Senior Dispenser, Tseung Kwan O Hospital

StudentSharing.Jarvis Yiu: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Baking and Pastry Arts, ICI

StudentSharing.Jonathan Lopes: Front Office Supervisor, Kowloon Shangri-La, Hong Kong

VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR

Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
