Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Applicants are required to pass a basic physical assessment.
Articulation: Graduates may pursue relevant top-up Degrees locally or overseas. They may articulate to the top-up Degree programme offered by the Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BSSc (Hons) in Sports Coaching BSSc (Hons) in Sports and Recreation Management They may also apply for admission to the top-up Degree programme in Hong Kong offered by the following university in collaboration with the School for Higher and Professional Education (SHAPE): University of Lancashire, UK: BA (Hons) in Sport and Physical Education # # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for the registered course no.
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed in sports coaching, exercise instruction, physical education training, physical fitness training, and sports supervision in public and private organisations, such as schools, community centres, National Sports Associations, Leisure and Cultural Services Department, Non-governmental Organisations, private sports and recreation clubs, sports and recreation programme and event planning companies, sports marketing consultancy firms and sports media companies.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Customer Service Fundamentals , Individual Sports I , Team Sports I , Functional Anatomy and Exercise Physiology , Fitness Coaching & Management I (Year Long Module across Semesters 1 & 2) , Exercise Nutrition , Coaching Major I , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness
Curriculum.Semester 2:
Sports Injury Management , Individual Sports II , Outdoor Sports I , Fitness Coaching & Management I (Year Long Module across Semesters 1 & 2) , Coaching Major II , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Year Long Module across
              Semesters 2 & 3) , IT Essentials – Hospitality
Curriculum.Semester 3:
Motor Learning Project , Team Sports II , Outdoor Sports II , Aerobic Dance and Kick Boxing , Yoga and Pilates , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Year Long Module across
              Semesters 2 & 3) , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Exercise Principles and Programming Project , Sports Biomechanics , Sports Psychology , Fitness Coaching & Management II (Year Long Module across Semesters 4 & 5) , Coaching Major III , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 5: eSports , Sports Business Management , Research Methods in Social Science , Fitness Coaching & Management II (Year Long Module across Semesters 4 & 5) , Coaching Major Project , Exercise for Selected Population , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in International Hospitality and Tourism Management
ProfessionalRecognition: The programme is accredited by the Institute of Hospitality (IoH) of UK and the United Nations World Tourism Organisation (UNWTO).
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BA (Hons) in Hotel Operations Management BA (Hons) in Culinary Arts and Management BSSc (Hons) in Sports and Recreation Management Graduates may pursue relevant accredited Degree programmes offered by local or overseas institutions, e.g.: Northumbria University, UK: BSc (Hons) in International Tourism, Hospitality and Events # # It is a matter of discretion for individual employers to recognise any qualification to which this course may lead. Please refer to the corresponding programme page in the website of SHAPE for more details
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed at entry level in the hospitality industry. After accumulating further operational experiences, they can be promoted to supervisory or middle management positions, such as Assistant Front Office Manager, Assistant Restaurant Manager, Assistant Clubhouse Manager and Tour Consultant, etc.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Customer Service Fundamentals , Front Office Essentials , Introduction to Leisure and Recreation , Theme Parks Operations and Management , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Hospitality
Curriculum.Semester 2: Fundamentals of Event Tourism , Hospitality Marketing Management , Housekeeping Essentials , Bartending and Practices , Wine and Spirits Studies , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: Food Service in Practices , Clubhouse Management , International Culinary Fundamentals and Practices , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search
Curriculum.Semester 4: Applied Food and Beverage Projects , Local Experience and Culture , Front Office Supervision , Hospitality Accounting and Finance , Hospitality Human Resource Operation and Management , International Hospitality Business (Year Long Module across Semesters 4 & 5) , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 5: Tourism Transport Operations , Environmental Management in the Hospitality Industry , Hospitality Management , International Hospitality Business (Year Long Module across Semesters 4 & 5) , Leisure Leadership and Programming , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations and Promotional Text Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Enrichment Module A

ProfessionalRecognition:
Students may choose one of the following streams in Semester 3, subject to their choice, academic performance and availability of places in the stream, so as to enhance their professional knowledge and skills in the respective study area:

International Cuisine
Classic European Cuisine
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BA (Hons) in Culinary Arts and Management Graduates may pursue relevant accredited Degree programmes offered by local or overseas institutions, e.g.: University College Birmingham, UK: BA (Hons) in Culinary Arts Management or BA (Hons) in International Hospitality and Tourism Management* Northumbria University, UK: BSc (Hons) in International Tourism, Hospitality and Events* * Applicants should meet the English language requirement set out by the University, i.e. HKDSE English Language at Level 3 or above (with all components at Level 3 or above) / IELTS with an overall score of 6.0 or above (with all components at score 5.5 or above) or equivalent (Interview may be required for certain programmes)
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as operative positions in the Western culinary industries available in a wide range of catering establishments such as five-star hotels, upscale restaurants, exclusive clubhouses, large theme parks and more.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Introduction to Management and Organisations , Food Hygiene & Safety , Food & Beverage Service , European Cuisine in Practice , International Cuisine in Practice , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Whole Person Development - MindShift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness
Curriculum.Semester 2: Human Resource Management , Baking & Pastry , Italian Cuisine , South American Cuisine , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , IT Essentials: Hospitality , Whole Person Development - Collaboration Teamwork & Social Engagement , Enrichment Module A
Curriculum.Semester 3: Accounting for Hospitality Management , Menu Planning & Development , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Menu Operations
Curriculum.Semester 4: Services Marketing , Kitchen Management , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace , Classic European Cuisine , Modern Cold Preparations
Curriculum.Semester 5: Law and Ethics for Catering Operations , Wine & Spirit Fundamentals , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Food Nutrition for Chefs , Enrichment Module B , Innovative Culinary Project , Contemporary European Cuisine

StudentSharing: ICI programme has endowed me with invaluable experience and training that have been instrumental in my career development. The comprehensive curriculum, which covers a wide range of trade-specific skills and knowledge have helped me to be well-versed in business operation in the catering industry.

StudentSharing: ICI’s programme combines theory and practical application, enabling me to master the knowledge and skills of pastry and sugar art while stimulating my creative thinking. StudentSharing.Jarvis Yiu: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Baking and Pastry Arts, ICI

StudentSharing: The IVE Higher Diploma programme offers various opportunities for internships, exchanges, and industry collaborations. During my studies, I engaged in an exchange programme with a French winery for winemaking and hands-on experience with local, seasoned catering professionals. This valuable experience paved the way to join a five-star hotel group, where I could effectively apply my knowledge and skills.

StudentSharing.Jonathan Lopes: Front Office Supervisor, Kowloon Shangri-La, Hong Kong

StudentSharing: The IVE Higher Diploma fully equipped me for my career. During my studies, I obtained various professional certifications and had the opportunity to intern at major sports events, which was highly beneficial. After graduation, I successfully progressed to Bachelor’s Degree at a UK university, pursuing a career in the sports and recreation industry.

Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BSc (Hons) in Information and Communications Technology BSc (Hons) in Cyber Security Graduates may apply for admission to the following programmes: Degree programmes in local universities: The City University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Baptist University The Chinese University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Polytechnic University The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology The Education University of Hong Kong The University of Hong Kong Articulation Degree programmes in overseas universities (conducted at IVE campus): The Coventry University, UK University of the West of England, UK Other Universities in the UK and Australia
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as Network Engineers and Administrators in the areas of telecommunication systems / networks installation, maintenance, operation and administration. For the contemporary telecommunication systems, they are also equipped with the software development skills to work for the services implementation. They may also be employed in technical marketing as well as technical support.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Introduction to Programming , Network Fundamentals , Discrete Mathematics and Statistics , Operating Systems Fundamentals , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness
Curriculum.Semester 2: IT Essentials - Web , Database Principles , Object Oriented Programming , Networking Technologies , Signals and Systems , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part A) , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 3: Telecommunications and Networking Project , Web Application Development Frameworks , IT Professionalism , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part B) , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
Curriculum.Semester 4: Telecommunication Network Practice (Part 1) , Communication Systems , Advanced Networking Technologies , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing
Curriculum.Semester 5: Telecommunication Network Practice (Part 2) , Telecommunication Network Services , Wireless Communications , Contemporary Topics in Telecommunications and Networking , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module A

StudentSharing: (Chinese version only) 電訊及網絡科技高級文憑課程教授各種網絡技能的應用，還提供良好的學習體驗。在教師的支持下，我參加了VTC職學計劃，並在一家網絡公司實習。通過在職場上學到的知識，應用到畢業專題項目中，從而大大提升了我的學術表現。畢業後，升讀了理工大學電子計算（榮譽）理學士三年級。希望通過不同的工作機會，裝備自己以回饋社會。

StudentSharing.Lau Kong Lung: Graduate, Higher Diploma in Telecommunications and Networking

StudentSharing: The VTC programme focuses on teaching industry-standard systems and technologies, with a strong emphasis on practicality. It also equips students to obtain industry-recognised certifications, ensuring their knowledge and skills meet international standards. The VTC Higher Diploma opened the door to further studies, enabling me to progress smoothly to Bachelor’s and Master’s Degrees at The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology.

StudentSharing.Michael Chung: Cyber Security Architect of a global Cybersecurity Company

StudentSharing: If I had not enrolled in the Higher Diploma in Software Engineering and regained my interest in Information Technology, I would have never had the opportunity to study for a Bachelor's Degree in the UK, then directly to a Doctorate, and conduct research in programming languages and compilers, let alone join Huawei to develop my career. I hope that in the future, I can apply my research results to the company's cutting-edge communication technology projects to benefit society.

StudentSharing.Steven Cheung: Principal Engineer, Huawei Technology R&D (UK) Ltd

StudentSharing: After graduation, I chose to pursue further studies at a local university. My prior experience provided a strong basis for my technical growth. I obtained professional knowledge during my time there, engaging in competitions and projects under mentorship to improve my practical skills. In university, I continued this spirit of exploration, actively participating in competitions and winning numerous awards. I decided to establish a company to promote the integration of education and creative technology. The company received funding from the Hong Kong Science and Technology Parks (HKSTP) and has won several awards in both international and local competitions.

StudentSharing: At IVE, I acquired solid foundational knowledge in cloud management and network security. Subsequently, I obtained the AWS Solutions Architect Professional certification, and I am now leveraging these skills to manage cloud operations within a financial institution, leveraging my professional expertise.

My interest in computer-related subjects started when I was young. After completing the Diploma of Foundation Studies at IVE, I chose to progress to Higher Diploma. Through diverse learning modes and internship opportunities, I gained practical experience and deepened my industry knowledge, eventually progressing to a local university to pursue my ideal profession.

The VTC programme focuses on teaching industry-standard systems and technologies, with a strong emphasis on practicality. It also equips students to obtain industry-recognised certifications, ensuring their knowledge and skills meet international standards. The VTC Higher Diploma opened the door to further studies, enabling me to progress smoothly to Bachelor’s and Master’s Degrees at The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology.

StudentSharing: The Higher Diploma in Cloud and Data Centre Administration is closely aligned with industry needs. In addition to learning about cloud computing systems, students will also study virtualization systems and data center management, covering a comprehensive range of topics. Practical training helps students apply professional knowledge, aiding in the successful acquisition of professional certifications. The course content includes multiple certifications from AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud, making it a gateway to the IT cloud computing industry. Many of my classmates have secured jobs at renowned multinational IT companies right after graduation, even without a degree! Large enterprises are eager for talent with cloud certifications. I am very honored to work at Amazon, the world's largest company, and I am aiming to become a cloud solutions architect.

StudentSharing.Law Mei Ching Pearly Jean: Solution Architect Technical Interns, Amazon Web Servicces

StudentSharing: Joining the Higher Diploma in Artificial Intelligence and Mobile Applications Development has greatly expanded my knowledge and experiences. During my studies, I was lucky to intern at Cathay Pacific and take part in tech competitions, using what I learned in class. Powered by AI technologies, I won many local and regional awards. After graduating, I moved on to study Computer Science at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology. Along with my classmates, we got startup funding and started our own company, balancing studies and business. I am thankful for the strong foundation that has been laid for my future.

I am very happy to have chosen the Higher Diploma in Artificial Intelligence and Mobile Applications Development. This experience has given me a strong foundation for my career. During my studies, I received the HKSAR Scholarship for Outstanding Performance and after graduating, I went on to study Computer Science at the City University of Hong Kong. Using the skills I learned from the higher diploma programme, I represented Hong Kong, China in the WorldSkills Competition and won a medallion of excellence. I also won the Champion with my team at the Cathay Pacific Hackathon. After finishing university, I applied my knowledge in mobile applications development and AI to my work, putting what I learned into practice. I am grateful to HKIIT for helping me build a solid start for my future.

StudentSharing.Lee Chak Kei, Jack: Mobile Application Developer, International Fintech Company

StudentSharing: At IVE, I acquired solid foundational knowledge in cloud management and network security. Subsequently, I obtained the AWS Solutions Architect Professional certification, and I am now leveraging these skills to manage cloud operations within a financial institution, leveraging my professional expertise.

My interest in computer-related subjects started when I was young. After completing the Diploma of Foundation Studies at IVE, I chose to progress to Higher Diploma. Through diverse learning modes and internship opportunities, I gained practical experience and deepened my industry knowledge, eventually progressing to a local university to pursue my ideal profession.

The VTC programme focuses on teaching industry-standard systems and technologies, with a strong emphasis on practicality. It also equips students to obtain industry-recognised certifications, ensuring their knowledge and skills meet international standards. The VTC Higher Diploma opened the door to further studies, enabling me to progress smoothly to Bachelor’s and Master’s Degrees at The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology.

StudentSharing.Michael Chung: Cyber Security Architect of a global Cybersecurity Company

ProgrammeName: Higher Diploma in AI and Smart Technology
ProfessionalRecognition: The programme is recognised by the Hong Kong Computer Society as satisfying the academic requirements for its Associate Membership.
EntranceRequirements: HKDSE: {'Chinese Language': '2', 'English Language': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject1': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject2': '2', 'Core/Elective Subject3': '2'}
VTC Diploma of Foundation Studies; OR
VTC Diploma of Vocational Education / VTC Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate; OR
Diploma Yi Jin / Diploma of Applied Education; OR

Relevant and substantial work experience and/or prior qualification(s) obtained in or outside Hong Kong (e.g. assessed through recognition of prior learning and credit accumulation and transfer), with demonstrated ability to undertake sub-degree education; OR
Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may articulate to the Degree programmes offered by Technological and Higher Education Institute of Hong Kong (THEi): BSc (Hons) in Information and Communications Technology BEng (Hons) in Digital Manufacturing (Medium of Instruction: Chinese (Putonghua)) Graduates may apply for admission to the following programmes: Degree programmes in local universities: The City University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Baptist University The Chinese University of Hong Kong The Education University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Polytechnic University The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology The Hong Kong Metropolitan University The University of Hong Kong Articulation Degree programmes in overseas universities (conducted at IVE campus): The Coventry University, UK University of the West of England, UK Other Universities in the UK and Australia
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as IoT Application Engineer, Artificial Intelligence Engineer, System and Network Administrator (Cybersecurity) and Cybersecurity Analyst.

Curriculum.Semester 1: Programming Fundamentals , Network Fundamentals , Database Applications , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Web
Curriculum.Semester 2: IoT Fundamentals , Wireless Technologies , Advanced Scripting Technology , Discrete Mathematics and Statistics , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement
Curriculum.Semester 3: AI Cloud Services , Web Technologies , System Administration and IoT Project , Enrichment Module B
Curriculum.Semester 4: Artificial Intelligence Applications , Big Data Analytics , Cybersecurity - Part I , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing
Curriculum.Semester 5: Enrichment Module A , Cybersecurity - Part II , IT Professionalism , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace

StudentSharing: During the two years of studying for the Higher Diploma in AI and Smart Technology, I gained a lot, such as increasing my understanding of artificial intelligence and training my problem-solving skills during the programming process. This programme provided internship opportunities, allowing me to better understand the working environment and apply the knowledge I learned in the workplace. I am very grateful for the careful guidance of the teachers, which enabled me to be admitted to the University of Hong Kong (HKU) upon graduation.

StudentSharing: (Chinese version only) 數據科學課程實用有趣，在老師們的循循善誘下，我取得優異成績，並獲得多間大學錄取。在高級文憑這兩年中，我積極參加本地和全國比賽，並取得出色成績，從而吸引資金贊助自己團隊研發科技項目。我現在於香港寬頻任職高級商業分析師，積極參與並領導公司的大型IT項目，運用課程的專業知識和技能，開發全新智能分析系統來提升運營效率。

Equivalent

Applicants can use HKDSE Applied Learning (ApL) subjects (except ApL Chinese) for admission purpose. An Attained, Attained with Distinction (I) and Attained with Distinction (II) in an ApL subject (Category B subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2, Level 3 and Level 4 respectively for admission purpose.

For 2024 HKDSE and before, a Grade D or E and a Grade C or above in an HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) are regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 and Level 3 respectively. From 2025 HKDSE onwards, Language Proficiency Level A2 or above in French / German / Spanish, N3 or above in Japanese and TOPIK II: Grade 3 or above in Korean are also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. Recognition of Urdu Language subject in 2026 HKDSE Other Language subject (Category C subjects) will be announced later. For details, please click here. One Other Language subject can be counted for admission purpose.
An Attained in HKDSE Citizenship and Social Development is regarded as equivalent to an HKDSE subject at Level 2 for admission purpose.

If one of the five HKDSE subjects is Citizenship and Social Development, the general entrance requirement would become Attained for Citizenship and Social Development and Level 2 or above in four other HKDSE subjects including Chinese Language and English Language. Besides, Level 2 or above in Mathematics Extended Part (Module 1 or Module 2) is also recognised as one of the five HKDSE subjects in fulfilling the general entrance requirement. If applicants have taken both Module 1 and Module 2, either one of the modules (with a higher level) will be counted for admission purpose.

Applicable to holders of Diploma in Vocational Education / Diploma of Vocational Education (DVE) (students admitted to DVE in AY2017/18 or before are required to complete prescribed further studies modules).
Upon completion of the Diploma of Vocational Baccalaureate (DVB) programmes, students can progress to Higher Diploma programmes in VTC, subject to their BTEC and IGCSE results.
Applications with work experience and/or prior qualification(s) submitted will be assessed individually by the departments concerned.
Articulation: Graduates may apply for admission to the following programmes: Degree programmes in local universities: The City University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Baptist University The Chinese University of Hong Kong The Hong Kong Polytechnic University The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology The Hong Kong Metropolitan University The University of Hong Kong Articulation Degree programmes in overseas universities (conducted at IVE campus): University of the West of England, UK Other Universities in the UK and Australia
CareerProspects: Graduates may be employed as designers, technical managers, operators and technicians in theme parks, professional theatre companies and theatre venues.

Curriculum.Semester 1: CAD Study , Entertainment Technologies and Professional Practices , Sound Technology in Theme Park and Theatre , Lighting Technology in Theme Park and Theatre , Digital Photography & Video Shooting Fundamentals , Digital Video Editing Fundamentals , Essential Workplace Communication: Effective Oral Interaction , Whole Person Development - Mindshift: Achieving Personal Growth & Effectiveness , IT Essentials - Media
Curriculum.Semester 2: Sound Design and Operation , Stage Lighting Design and Operation , Video Production and Stage Visual Effects , Interactive Media Programming , Mathematics and Problem Solving Techniques , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Conversation and Reports , Essential Workplace Communication: Digital Written Correspondence , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part A)
Curriculum.Semester 3: Entertainment Project Development , Live Entertainment Event Broadcasting , Enrichment Module B , Professional Workplace Communication: Storytelling and Job Search , Whole Person Development - Collaboration, Teamwork & Social Engagement (Part B)
Curriculum.Semester 4: Live Event Management and Strategies , Integrated Theme Park and Theatre Entertainment Study , Final Year Project , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
Curriculum.Semester 5: Audio-Visual Installation and Technical Assembling , IT Professionalism , Final Year Project , Vocational Chinese Communication: Putonghua Presentations, Administrative and Technical Text Writing , Professional Workplace Communication: Pitching and Persuasive Presentation , Enrichment Module A

Curriculum.Semester 4: Final Year Project , Digital Music and Sound Design , Art Technology with AI , 3D Visualization for Multimedia and VR , Immersive Experience Design , Professional Workplace Communication: Proposal and Report Writing , Whole Person Development - Enhancing Competencies in the 21st Century Workplace
